title: Codegen Is Not Productivity
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 13 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.antifound.com%2Fposts%2Fcodegen-is-not-productivity%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdevops/1/0100019cf6667964-8c98915b-deda-4147-ac4b-0b834156e1e2-000000/Ora4uppvCsl-P3sITPJyLcWmD4FvfMWq99U5s6KutN0=448

summary: Bridging the enterprise AI context gap using internal knowledge layers and RAG (7 minute read) Foundation models lack institutional knowledge, causing hallucinations in enterprise settings. Integrating Stack Overflow Internal with OpenAI via RAG allows companies like Uber to provide human-validated, traceable answers. This context layer ensures AI accuracy and production-readiness by grounding responses in verified internal documentation and architectural history.

title: Stablecoins and the Rewiring of Global Finance
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FTw9n7u/1/0100019cf6c5d009-dff7e909-37cb-4f85-87f5-54cccefe5f58-000000/hJ8dsUi9qOW9KZPtZRM1VBf_UpMG7U_e_6ntsv9VOHI=448
summary: Stablecoins and the Rewiring of Global Finance (10 minute read) For decades, cross-border payments have been one of the slowest-moving areas of global finance. Despite the digital transformation of banking, international payments have remained fragmented, expensive, and slow. Settlement can take days, liquidity must be pre-funded across jurisdictions, and compliance layers add complexity. Stablecoins and regulated digital assets are beginning to challenge that model.

title: Can LLMs Be Computers?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 20 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.percepta.ai%2Fblog%2Fcan-llms-be-computers%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019cf6d18831-ebabc6e9-0e4d-4b23-969a-ad2f2a18c962-000000/qsX1lafOnPZ9bsFQ0TJ4pM1MJTXN2Aek5S5GgoPYkAA=448
summary: Can LLMs Be Computers? (20 minute read) Transformers can execute programs efficiently inside their own inference loop. This opens a path towards AI systems that integrate learned representations with compiled algorithms inside a single computational substrate. Solving humanity's toughest problems will require systems that can both reason flexibly and compute reliably. Future AI systems will have software as part of the model.

title: MCP is Dead; Long Live MCP!
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-16, Reading time: 20 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fchrlschn.dev%2Fblog%2F2026%2F03%2Fmcp-is-dead-long-live-mcp%2F%3Futm_source=tldrai/1/0100019cf6d18831-ebabc6e9-0e4d-4b23-969a-ad2f2a18c962-000000/3J9Dr-Wfz2jjC-bcbwamI80FBVWu4bzEPGK-klUrI68=448
summary: MCP is Dead; Long Live MCP! (20 minute read) The current industry zeitgeist is dialed in on CLIs, just like it was on MCP a few short months ago. Using CLI can result in token savings, but custom CLIs run into the same context problems as MCP, except without structure and many other sacrifices. Individual usage of coding agents looks very different from organizational adoption of coding agents. MCP is the present and future for enterprise and org-level use cases.

title: The Five Categories of World Models

summary: The Five Categories of World Models (5 minute read) AMI Labs and World Labs both raised over $1B on "world models," but the term covers five distinct approaches: JEPA, spatial intelligence, learned simulation, physical AI infrastructure, and active inference. Curated context needs agents with the right information without overwhelming them, and fast feedback loops help to iterate. Each layer is necessary, and none alone is sufficient.

title: The Future Of Software Engineering with Anthropic
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-17, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.akashbajwa.co%2Fp%2Fthe-future-of-software-engineering%3Futm_source=tldrnewsletter/1/0100019cfb52ce55-2d5ebed3-7c0b-44d0-8264-25905be4fbc8-000000/onBmG_QbvPsqT-NWNhNDftzbBjnasCq1lClbX-WX740=448
summary: The Future Of Software Engineering with Anthropic (12 minute read) Better coding tools produce better models, which produce better coding tools. The bottleneck has moved to managing long-horizon tasks and deploying agents in regulated settings. The human role is shifting to planning, evaluating, and steering. Enterprise adoption is gated by permissioning, sandboxing, and regulatory caution more than by model capability. Slop and convergence are real concerns, and context remains unsolved.

summary: The Base Builder's Playbook (5 minute read) Integrate Coinbase tooling early for both product improvement and ecosystem promotion, build revenue-generating businesses rather than projects (since vibecoding has eliminated the moat around shipping), leverage Base's distribution power over grants, and integrate ERC-8021 builder codes for onchain attribution ahead of Base's token launch. The ecosystem rewards traction and execution over requests and pitch decks.

summary: Amazon rolls out 1-hour, 3-hour delivery as ultrafast shipping trend grows in the US (3 minute read) Amazon is rolling out one-hour and three-hour deliveries in parts of the US. Three-hour delivery will be available in about 2,000 cities and towns, while one-hour delivery will be available in hundreds of areas. More than 90,000 products are eligible for the service. Shoppers will be able to filter search for products that can be delivered in three hours or less.

summary: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano on APIs (2 minute read) OpenAI has released GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano. GPT-5.4 mini is live on the API, Codex, and ChatGPT, while the nano model is reserved for API users only. Both models are designed for workflows where response time shapes the product. GPT-5.4 mini is priced at $0.75 per million input tokens and $4.50 per million output tokens, while GPT-5.4 nano lands at $0.20 and $1.25.

title: Why Node.js Needs a Virtual File System
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.platformatic.dev%2Fwhy-nodejs-needs-a-virtual-file-system%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d00a45175-42319023-7d4a-413d-9f0b-7cd9ded6b486-000000/EFmogqpp5N9lSnS4G4Rjr1qHv_0-0eJizHpVzzp5TrY=449
summary: Why Node.js Needs a Virtual File System (10 minute read) Node.js has long struggled with the inability to virtualize its file system. The new `node:vfs` module addresses this by providing a core Virtual File System that hooks directly into both the `fs` API and Node.js's module resolver. This VFS allows applications to access in-memory or embedded assets, allowing for better isolated test environments, secure multi-tenant file access, and efficient handling of dynamically generated code.

They also built a centralized MCP server called Toolshed with 500 tools, shared across all agents.

summary: If It Quacks Like a Package Manager (5 minute read) Any tool with transitive dependencies, where A depends on B, which depends on C, has effectively become a package manager, whether it calls itself one or not. GitHub Actions, Ansible Galaxy, Terraform modules, and Helm have all crossed this line and inherited the full set of supply chain problems, such as mutable tags, no lockfiles, and unverified transitive deps.

title: Edge.js
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 12 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwasmer.io%2Fposts%2Fedgejs-safe-nodejs-using-wasm-sandbox%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d00a45175-42319023-7d4a-413d-9f0b-7cd9ded6b486-000000/3gN4mZ3K764xjN0K4PNkTWwtuV9X5BcXAuBMiRk_1Ls=449
summary: Edge.js (12 minute read) Edge.js is a new JavaScript runtime designed to safely run Node.js workloads for AI and Edge computing. It differentiates itself from other edge runtimes by preserving full Node.js compatibility, isolating unsafe system calls and native modules through WebAssembly (WASIX) while running the JavaScript engine natively. This architecture allows existing Node.js applications and native modules to run unmodified in a secure, sandboxed, serverless environment.

title: Does splitting work across AI agents actually save time? I tested it
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.logrocket.com%2Fsplitting-across-ai-agents%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019d00a45175-42319023-7d4a-413d-9f0b-7cd9ded6b486-000000/6P7G3BkPlKgSw5smJXayuSoY9XL1J1_w50p3SN8f3QE=449
summary: Does splitting work across AI agents actually save time? I tested it (6 minute read) Five multi-agent setups were benchmarked on the same task. Cursor subagents won (12 min, all tests passing), Agent Teams cut cost 70% vs solo, and the OpenAI SDK finished in 90 seconds for $0.009 but failed every single test because the agent didn't read enough of the codebase before writing code. More agents only helps when the task genuinely parallelizes, and agents can agree on interfaces before work starts.

title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FLJyXpy/1/0100019d01111325-105e9549-6cac-4747-8f33-eb37f0e58595-000000/rQB0n8LUn58JWwohGkH9Rky-thp-Bu5QBmT9W2eEs1s=449
summary: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish (4 minute read) Attackers sent a JPMorgan-themed phishing email to an Outpost24 executive that passed DKIM checks and existing email security controls. The link chained through Cisco Secure Web, Nylas tracking, a compromised Indian firm, an expired-reused domain, and a Cloudflare-hosted site before a Microsoft 365 credential page.

title: Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Others United Under New Anti-Scam Pact
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 2 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FbcCf0d/1/0100019d01111325-105e9549-6cac-4747-8f33-eb37f0e58595-000000/Bz4FkoePH4vU3nFlwR6M4cAg1_K-KA84h9NEr9LO79s=449
summary: Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and Others United Under New Anti-Scam Pact (2 minute read) Ahead of the UN Global Fraud Summit in Austria, eleven tech platforms, including Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and OpenAI, joined to create the new Online Services Accord Against Scams. The agreement involves the platforms agreeing to share intelligence and best practices about threats on their platforms and integrate defensive tools. The pact is voluntary and does not include any enforcement measures.

title: New ClickFix attack leverages WorkFlowy for stealthy malware delivery
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 1 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FykVcjo/1/0100019d01111325-105e9549-6cac-4747-8f33-eb37f0e58595-000000/gAeNUZ5wQlojjlV5Mg2SEKNjOI2pQ9PpxFVfZS08QDQ=449
summary: New ClickFix attack leverages WorkFlowy for stealthy malware delivery (1 minute read) Atos researchers identified a ClickFix variant that trojanizes WorkFlowy's .asar archive to execute a C2 beacon with user privileges before the legitimate application initializes, bypassing Microsoft Defender for Endpoint by avoiding scripting engines and living-off-the-land binaries.

title: Microsoft PhotoDNA is vulnerable to false positives and data leakage
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-03-18, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fcyberinsider.com%2Fmicrosoft-photodna-is-vulnerable-to-false-positives-and-data-leakage%2F%3Futm_source=tldrinfosec/1/0100019d01111325-105e9549-6cac-4747-8f33-eb37f0e58595-000000/zRmkHDtN8uQMld3ef1J8B_ZXi7e35sX39bPAu0h5qao=449
summary: Microsoft PhotoDNA is vulnerable to false positives and data leakage (3 minute read) Researchers from Ghent University and KU Leuven published the first white-box analysis of Microsoft PhotoDNA, revealing that its piecewise-linear hash function can be exploited within seconds on a standard laptop to produce exact collisions, generate false positives matching CSAM hashes, evade detection, and partially reconstruct source images.
